Kokkos-based High-Accuracy Relativistic Magnetohydrodynamics with AMR

KHARMA is an implementation of the HARM scheme for general relativistic magnetohydrodynamics (GRMHD) in C++. It is based on the Parthenon AMR framework, using Kokkos for parallelism and GPU support. It is composed of modular "packages," which in theory make it easy to add or swa…

A C++ library to compute neighborhood information for point clouds within a fixed radius. Suitable for many applications, e.g. neighborhood search for SPH fluid simulations.

Kratos Multiphysics (A.K.A Kratos) is a framework for building parallel multi-disciplinary simulation software. Modularity, extensibility and HPC are the main objectives. Kratos has BSD license and is written in C++ with extensive Python interface.
